title: Module 7 knowledge check
markdown: Six questions on scams, trust, and next steps. Unlimited attempts.

questions.prompt: Which is a common scam red flag?
choices: Urgent pressure to pay with gift cards and keep it secret
A written syllabus from a .gov education site
Checking your free credit report annually
Comparing APYs on insured savings accounts

questions.prompt: A trustworthy place for U.S. consumer money education is:
choices: CFPB (consumerfinance.gov)
An unsolicited DM promising guaranteed riches
A stranger asking for your SSN in chat
A pop-up that demands remote access now

questions.prompt: Identity theft means someone uses your personal information without permission to commit fraud.
choices: True
False

questions.prompt: If you get a scary message claiming to be your bank, you should:
choices: Contact the bank using a phone number or app you know is official
Immediately send payment to the message's instructions
Share your password so they can "verify" you
Ignore all future account monitoring

questions.prompt: A simple financial plan should include at least:
choices: A SMART goal, budget snapshot, emergency target, next money step, and a fraud-protection habit
Only stock tips
Only your SSN
Only a list of influencers
Content is educational and not personalized financial/tax/legal advice
The course files your taxes for you
The course guarantees investment returns
You must buy a featured product
